help.title: Help
help.dashboard_text: The Dashboard provides an overview of the system health. You can see the current status of the API and use quick access cards to navigate to important features like Template Management or Email Sending.
help.templates_text: In the Templates section, you can manage your email templates. Templates are identified by a unique ID and can be tagged for better organization. Each template supports a Subject, HTML content, and a Plain text version. Templates can come from three sources: Database (editable via this UI), File (read-only files on disk), and Git (read-only, synced from a Git repository). Filter the list by source or custom tags using the tag filter. Switch between grid and table view with the toggle in the toolbar. Read-only templates (File and Git) cannot be modified via the UI.
help.send_text: The Send Email page allows you to send emails in two ways: 'By Template' uses an existing template from your library, while 'Inline' lets you define the email content directly. You can specify recipients, CC/BCC, and a JSON model to fill variables in your templates.
help.render_text: Use the Render Preview to test how your templates look with actual data. Select a template and provide a JSON model. The system will render the email and show you a live preview in the browser.
help.auth_text: Access to the Admin Console supports two modes. In OIDC mode the login is handled via an external identity provider (e.g. Keycloak) and your user data appears in the top bar. In API-Key mode you enter a key on the login page; the system then determines which tenants you have access to. In both cases you can logout via the user menu in the top bar.
help.tenants_text: The Tenants section (Admin only) lets you manage the tenants of this installation. Each tenant has its own SMTP configuration, optional Git-Sync settings for templates, and can be activated or deactivated. Sensitive values like SMTP passwords and Git tokens are encrypted at rest and never returned in plain text by the API.
help.persistence_text: Your work is never lost. All form data is automatically stored in your browser while you type. The status of expanded or collapsed areas (accordions) is also preserved, so you can continue exactly where you left off on your next visit.
help.sample_text: Use the 'Sample' links above the input fields (HTML, JSON, Text) to quickly load test data. This is particularly helpful when designing new templates or testing the render preview.
help.highlight_text: For a better overview, all code editors (HTML and JSON) feature intelligent syntax highlighting. This helps you to identify errors in your templates or data models more quickly.
help.freemarker_text: Email templates are written in FreeMarker syntax. Use ${variableName} to insert values from the JSON model you provide at send or render time. Loops and conditionals are also supported: use <#list items as item>…</#list> to iterate over a list and <#if condition>…</#if> for conditional output. Built-in functions like ${name?upper_case} or ${amount?string["0.00"]} let you format values inline. The Subject and Plain Text fields support the same syntax as the HTML body.
